  • Visual workflow design and automation
  • Integration with SAP and third-party systems
  • Pre-built bot templates and AI services
  • Monitoring and analytics for process optimization

Example Use Case: Automate the end-to-end invoice processing workflow, from data extraction to approval, reducing manual intervention and accelerating processing times.

Comparison of SAP Build Tools

 

Conclusion and Next Steps

SAP BTP offers a powerful set of tools — SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation — to help organizations enhance employee experiences, rapidly develop applications, and automate processes. Start by identifying key areas where these tools can provide the most value, and consider initiating a small pilot project to explore their potential benefits. In the next blog, we’ll discuss advanced integration techniques and best practices for maximizing your SAP BTP investments.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps now returns attributes with process instances
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps has flow functions to connect with your process from SAP Build Process Automation. It used to be you had to retrieve a process's instances, and then for each one run a flow function to retrieve that instance’s attributes. Now, the "Retrieve Instances" flow functions returns the attributes with the instances.

Apparently this change was made a while ago, but I just found out by accident. Generally, this makes the "Get Process Attributes" flow function redundant, but it might still be useful to have.

So If I have a process with the following attributes:

If I now run the "Retrieve Instances" flow function.

I will get the attributes along with the instance info.

entry.title: SAP at Hackfestival 2024 - Technology & Sustainability Coming Together to Find Innovative Solutions
entry.content:
The Hackfestival 2024 is a highly anticipated tech event that took place in Mannheim between September 6-8. Under the motto “Decarbonize the Planet”, the festival aimed to bring together tech enthusiasts, sustainability experts, coders, and hackers to collaborate and innovate in sustainability related hands-on challenges during a full-weekend hackathon.  

Located at Congress Center Rosengarten, it combined a competitive hackathon and free citizen track with a wide range of engaging activities for the greater public - including an exhibition area, workshops, open discussions and seminars. Participants had the opportunity to network with other tech talents and industry leaders, share their ideas, grow their career, test their skills and learn from each other. 

Apart from that, the event was also a great opportunity to bring citizens closer to sustainability in general as well as individual corporate initiatives. As an event partner, we had many valuable exchanges around SAP’s own journey to net-zero and the SAP Sustainability product portfolio at our booth. It is always meaningful to see the interest in this topic, make a contribution and provide deeper insights into corporate sustainability and thus make it more tangible for a larger audience. 

SAP team at our SAP Sustainability boothSome key facts and figures around the Hackfestival: 

  • Organized by The Hackathon Company – an independent European hackathon-as-a-service provider  
  • 300+ participants 
  • 35+ partners (Google, aws, Roche and more) 
  • 48-hours of hacking 
  • Six sustainability challenges 
  • Price pool of 10.000+ Euros 

The event was kicked-off on Friday with an opening ceremony and inspiring speech by Alexander Gerst, who talked about his journey as an ESA astronaut followed by Christian Specht, mayor of the city of Mannheim. Gunther Rothermel, co-General Manager and Chief Product Officer for SAP Sustainability, and other industry channel partners were part of a fruitful panel discussion, giving insights about the corporate view on sustainability and how technology and innovation can help take climate action and make a difference in the world.  

Gunther said: “With increasing regulatory landscapes and reporting requirements, our customers have set themselves a broad set of challenging sustainability targets...and we did the same at SAP. There is a lot we can address with software and smart approaches to embed sustainability into digital business processes...First we wanted to start by acting as an exemplar, working on our own emissions and journey to net-zero as a company...in the second step, we help our customers to become more sustainable and accompany them with our SAP Sustainability products. Imagine that SAP customers generate 87% of total global commerce....By integrating sustainability more naturally into customers transactions, we can have a huge impact on decarbonization, reduction of waste and more together. So, both these sides matter to us.”  

The panel talk has clearly shown - at SAP, we incorporate sustainability in our strategy as well as our core values and are proud to have a strong internal SAP Community that engages in voluntary projects and lives sustainability in its daily actions, because every contribution, no matter how small, can change things. 

According to this discussion, the focus of our sustainability effort is based on four key areas:  

  • Taking Climate Action and Reducing Carbon Emissions 
  • Establishing a Circular Economy to reuse material and avoid waste 
  • Integrating a Social Responsibility dimension to drive socially responsible value chains, promote respect for human rights, and create positive societal impacts.
  • Holistic Steering & Reporting: Acting on the operational layer to be compliant and safe on the production and plant level as well as in company's own transactions and processes to be transparent on the way to net-zero. 

Sustainability @SAP

Afterwards the different challenges were introduced and kicked off by the companies. The highly motivated participants had 48 hours to solve challenges provided by the “Challenge Setters” under the main topic “Decarbonize the Planet”. Divided into teams they started working on sustainability solutions, including design and business perspectives.  

Coding has started

SAP' challenge: „𝐇𝐞𝐲, 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐞’𝐬 𝐜𝐚𝐫𝐛𝐨𝐧 𝐢𝐧 𝐦𝐲 𝐥𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐡🍴”: 

We provided the following task: “As one-third of global emissions stem from the food system, imagine the impact that can be made by addressing this challenge head-on – especially when there’s the catering needs of over 110,000 employees. ​The challenge for the participants is to develop a digital solution that helps decarbonize the planet by reducing emissions within the food system or specifically targeting food waste.” 

The SAP Challenge

Heads were put together, many innovative ideas were developed and there were no limits to creativity. In the end, we received a lot of valuable ideas, and the following team was among the winners:  

SAP challenge team: "FrostGuardians": Food waste comes from the fact that everyone wastes food every day, so with the help of AI and a camera, the team developed a fridge that manages what's in the fridge, gives tips on food that goes with a recipe and is connected to a shopping list. 

SAP team and the winnersBig congratulations to the team and all other winners for the inspiring and creative solutions. 🏅

"FrostGuardians" the proud winners of the SAP challenge

One of the main goals of the festival was to foster creativity and problem-solving skills among participants and to encourage people to develop solutions with a positive social impact. Many attendees left the festival feeling inspired and motivated to continue their work in the tech industry. 

The Hackfestival 2024 in Mannheim was a fantastic opportunity for tech enthusiasts to come together and push the boundaries of innovation. It provided a platform where like-minded individuals could collaborate and drive positive change. 

It was a pleasure to support this event as a partner. A hackathon is a great opportunity to access collective knowledge, promote creativity and team spirit, resulting in digital applications and innovative ideas that sometimes can even be the start of something big. 

There is only one thing left to say - A big thank you goes to the organizational team and all participants. Only together we can make a difference. 🌍 🌱

Please see the full list of all other winners' solutions (shared by Oliver Bruemmer, Founder & CEO The Hackathon Company):
 
Trash Buddy by Stadt Mannheim/ Metropolregion Rhein-Neckar: Scan products you want to throw away and get tips on what you can do with them. Find other solutions and don’t only throw it away. 
 
Mannheim Green City Quest by Stadt Mannheim/ Metropolregion Rhein-Neckar: Playful knowledge transfer app that encourages young people to try out new ways of thinking and exploring the city in a sustainable way with geolocations and gamification elements. 
 
Rochy - The Carbon Coach by Roche : "Rochy" gives sustainability reminders and awards points when fulfilled and proven with pictures, e.g. plate eaten empty. Collected points lead to coupons or can be donated.  
Carbon Crasher by Schwarz IT KG: Machine learning app that recommends products based on your interests, such as products that save CO2 or have a short expiration date, with special prices, coupons and gamification. 
 
KAEFER by KION Group: Efficient LLM model with key features: dynamic scoring of models, prompt model complexity matching, ML-based prompt categorization, self-learning architecture interaction.

Learn more about SAP Sustainability and subscribe to weekly Sustainability Highlight Newsletter.

entry.title: SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Target Group

Anyone interested to know more about extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud using SAP Build

 

Background

What is Extensibility

When a customer has unique business requirements that are not fully catered by standard SAP S/4HANA then there is a need to extend the system. These extensions can be classified into 2 broad categories:

  • On-stack extensibility involves extending SAP S/4HANA directly within the core system using key user tools or ABAP.
  • Side-by-side extensibility uses SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to develop custom applications or integrations outside the core S/4HANA system, ensuring greater flexibility and upgrade safety.

What is SAP Build

SAP Build ( including SAP Build Code) is the default extension development toolset on SAP BTP.

Build Tools under low-code spectrum:

  • SAP Build Apps - Create Mobile and Web applications
  • SAP Build Process Automation - Automate business processes and workflows
  • SAP Build Work Zone - Create Digital Workspaces and business sites

Build Tools under Pro-code spectrum:

SAP Build Code - Use Business Application Studio(BAS) and Cloud Application programming Model(CAP) to create Applications. And lot more.

What challenges are we trying to address here

  • Not easy to identify the available extension options for a given S/4HANA application directly in the system
  • Not easy to determine the right extension points (eg APIs, Events) for a given S/4HANA application
  • Not an integrated experience when extending SAP S/4HANA in a side-by-side manner on BTP

 

Introduction

  • A new Extensibility wizard is available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 that provides both on-stack and side-by-side extension options consolidated into a single wizard.
  • It provides a guided experience to kick start extension creation and enables seamless navigation from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Build.
  • The wizard understands which S/4HANA application it has been started from and accordingly filters the extensions points( like API, Events) based on the business context.

This wizard can be accessed from any extensible SAP S/4HANA app as shown below. "Create Extension" option is available under the Me Area (User menu) on the top right.

On clicking the Create Extension option, the Extensibility Wizard opens up displaying the various extension options

Note: Setup steps mentioned next are needed as a prerequisite to be able to see the "Create Extension" option

 

Setup

Short summary of the setup steps:

  1. Subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation in your SAP BTP subaccount.
  2. Add your SAP S/4HANA cloud system in the BTP Global account under System landscape and register in a formation.
  3. Create 2 destinations in the BTP account pointing to UI service and API service of SAP S/4HANA. This is needed for communication from Build to SAP S/4HANA, for example to fetch business context, events and authorizations.
  4. Create a communication arrangement in SAP S/4HANA. This is to enable communication from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Build.
  5. Assign the required authorization in SAP S/4HANA to access the wizard.

Detailed setup steps:

  • BTP setup steps - link
  • S4 setup steps - link

 

Extension options in the Wizard ( Sep 2024 )

On-stack

Custom Field

Enables addition of new fields in desired business objects. The wizard opens the Custom Field App with the business contexts pre set.

 

Custom Logic

Enables addition of custom logic via Cloud BAdIs. Here as well the wizard opens the Custom Logic App with the business contexts pre set.

 

Side-by-Side

Custom Process

Create a Process in SAP Build Process Automation that starts based on an event from SAP S/4HANA. The business context of the starting SAP S/4HANA app is pre set in the wizard to easily identify the relevant events.

A detailed explanation of this process extension option is available in this blog from my colleague.

The extensibility wizard is expected to grow with more extension options in future.

 

Conclusion

The new SAP Build Extensibility wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a simple guided experience to create extensions both on-stack and side-by-side.

If you have access to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system, follow the setup steps and do explore the Extensibility Wizard !

entry.title: Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
The release of SAP Build's new extensibility wizard forSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release marks a significant step in streamlining the creation of on-stack extensions (within SAP S/4HANA Cloud) and side-by-side extensions (in SAP BTP) to help keep the core clean. The wizard, which can be accessed directly from any business application that is enabled for key user adaptation, allows key users and even developers to easily create custom process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) that can seamlessly integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. In this blog, we will explore the use cases for process extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how the new wizard is a game-changer for customers looking to enhance their business processes

Side-by-side extensions

Side-by-side extensions run outside of SAP S/4HANA Cloud (or other business application) but interact with the business applications using standard extension points like APIs, events, etc. Side-by-side extensions can technically run on any application platform, nevertheless SAP provides and recommends its dedicated platform for extension: SAP BTP.

What are process extensions

Many applications in SAP S/4HANA use SAP Business Workflow enabling preconfigured workflow scenarios to be reused in various situations. The scenarios can either be implemented without any changes or configured for your business processes by making minor adjustments. On top of that, Flexible workflows allow you to define condition-based workflows for the pre-configured approval processes. For example, the flexible workflow for purchase requisitions allows you to define one-step or multi-step approval processes for purchase requisitions according to your requirements. These are pre-delivered as part of the SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. However, your use case may go beyond a typical pre-delivered approval workflow which needs a side-by-side process extension. Here are some use case patterns which warrants a process extension in SAP BTP:

  • Processes that span across systems.
  • Processes that reach the users who are outside SAP S/4HANA.
  • Use certain capabilities from SAP BTP, like SAP AI Services.
  • Any other requirements that are not covered in the standard processes and workflows.

Example use case

Consider a specific use case where you want to make sure that every invoice created in the system has a reference to a purchase order without user intervention. There are multiple ways this can be achieved including a process extension. A side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (in SAP BTP) can check if a purchase order is assigned for every new supplier invoice created in the system. If not, then the process shall identify the right purchase order using SAP AI Services in SAP BTP like Document Information Extraction, Data Attribute Recommendation, Invoice Object Recommendation, SAP AI Core, etc. When the machine learning model could not find the right match, then it could inform the procurement department member for further action. This process extension would run alongside the standard procurement process, adding the necessary functionality without altering the original process.

Why SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation in SAP BTP with its rich development tools, integration capability and pre-built content is the recommended process extension platform for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. SAP Build Process Automation has 500+ pre-built content packages for extending SAP and non-SAP applications. Most of those content is free-to-use and can be accessed from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition 2408 release, the extensibility wizard streamlines the creation of process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation. The process created in SAP Build Process Automation is deployed & executable in SAP BTP and can be triggered automatically when a business event is observed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Creating a process extension

Let us see how easy it is to create a process extension with the new wizard.

Pre-requisite:

  • Setup SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh

A business event-based triggering of process is a capability of SAP Build Process Automation. It requires SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh to observe the events in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to initiate a process in SAP Build Process Automation. Refer to this blog for the detailed setup instruction.

  • You have the right business role and can access the extensibility wizard as described in previous blog of this series.

Procedure

  • From any application which is enabled for key user adaptation, open the 'Me Area' to choose the new 'Create Extension' option to launch the extensibility wizard.

Choose Process Extension. (If you do not have entitlement to SAP Build Process Automation in the connected SAP BTP tenant, then this option will be disabled with a 'Not subscribed' ribbon in the tile).

Based on the application from where the extension was triggered, the wizard automatically identifies the available extension points (in 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition, only business events are supported. More extension points is planned as future increments).

Choose Business Events, and proceed to next step.

The wizard also recognises the event objects that are relevant for the application and automatically applies a filter on the business object. Of course, you can override these filters and search any event that is needed for your use case. Select the appropriate event object and proceed to the next step.

All events of the selected event object are shown. Select the appropriate event and proceed to the next step.

Give a name and description for your extension project that will be created in SAP Build (SAP BTP). 

Confirm the changes in the Summary step or go back to edit any steps. This creates an extension project for based in the inputs given in the previous steps.

Once the project is created successfully, you can directly navigate to the project in SAP Build. 

When navigated into SAP Build (in SAP BTP), you can see a process automation project with a process created for you. 

When you open the process, you can see that the event selected in the wizard is automatically added. All the context from the selected business event is now available at your disposal. 

You can proceed to use the low-code process builder to create an extension process as needed. You can call any APIs like the SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP Cloud Integration models or any SAP BTP services, you can add a user task that creates a task in My Inbox or SAP Task Center, you can call an RPA bot automation, you can send notification emails, etc. Once the process is created, release a version and deploy the content into SAP BTP. 

When this process automation project is deployed in SAP BTP, it listens to the specific event in the connected SAP S/4HANA Cloud. When such an event is observed, it starts an instance of the process with the context that is available in the business event.

For more information on the lifecycle, refer to the help documentation of SAP Build Process Automation.

Key take aways

  • A brand-new wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is available with 2408 release to create on-stack and side-by-side extensions.
  • The wizard can be called from any application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition that is enabled for key user adaptation.
  • Discover the available process extension points based on the application that was used to start the wizard, through the concept of Business Context.
  • Create a side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Look-ahead plans

  • Management of side-by-side extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • More extension points for process extensions apart from the business event.
  • Enable the wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

entry.title: Performant table list in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Managing and displaying large amounts of data in an application tends to require a lot of consideration, as it is easy to make performance suffer. I recently built a very performant list page for a S/4 HANA Cloud endpoint with 65k+ records using SAP Build Apps which will become available in a template later on, but meanwhile I wanted to share with you my solution so you can already adopt it in your SAP Build Apps application, if you are having performance issues in your list view.

The list page I created has a table type list with sorting, filtering and paging.

Preview of the performance of the table list I created

The main secrets of its great performance are:

  1. When page mounts, it begins to load 50 first items from the database to have something to display while it loads all of the items in the background.
  2. Even when all data is loaded, only 50 items are shown by default, unless the user goes to show the next 50 items, in which case 100 items are shown, and so forth. The way this is done is important – there is a separate data variable that only contains the items shown (BusinessPartnersFiltered). The sorting and filtering is also done into this data variable from the data variable that contains all the data (A_BusinessPartners1).

The core of this logic is placed on the page's logic canvas. The main logic for loading the data happens when the page is mounted, but there are events for when the user wants to see more data (but all data has not loaded yet) and when the data on the page needs sorting/filtering.

Logic of the list page

The page variables and their purposes are the following:

  • dataLoadFail and dataLoadSuccess if the fetching of the full data has failed or succeeded, some logic is run differently, e.g. the "Show more" button will not trigger fetching the next 50 items if all data has already been loaded.
  • showSpinner and showFilterSpinner show spinners in the UI, and block some logic from running by disabling buttons etc.
  • columns list contains the columns, their titles, order and the name of the fields. I use this page variable to avoid needing to change multiple places if I want to change which columns are displayed, or if I would want to change columns dynamically.
  • pageIndex, pageSize, searchQuery, sortBy and sortDirection are used for the implementation of paging, filtering and sorting by column.

As for the logic with which the items from the list containing all the items into the filtered list that is used in the list, I concocted the following formula:

 

 

SLICE(ORDER(IF(!IS_EMPTY(pageVars.searchQuery), SELECT<partner>(data.A_BusinessPartner1, SOME<column>(pageVars.columns, CONTAINS(LOWERCASE(ENCODE_JSON(LOOKUP(partner, column.fieldName))), LOWERCASE(pageVars.searchQuery)))), data.A_BusinessPartner1), IF(!IS_EMPTY(pageVars.sortBy), ENCODE_JSON(LOOKUP(item, pageVars.sortBy)), index), pageVars.sortDirection), 0, pageVars.pageSize)

 

 

This formula first figures out if there is a filter used (= searchQuery is something other than empty), and if yes, it only selects the items from the master list that contain the search query in one of the fields used in the columns. If no, the full master list goes into the next stage. In the next stage it checks if there is a column to sort by (= sortBy is something other than empty), and sorts the filtered master list based on this field into the desired sortDirection. After that the page is cut into size, which is usually 50, unless the user has pressed the Show more button.

There are many additional small details that make me proud of this particular list page, but this concludes the main ones that contribute towards its great performance. If you have any further questions about creating table lists in SAP Build Apps, please comment below.

entry.title: Build Process Automation Notifications Part 1 of 3 - Default Notifications
entry.content:
In September 2024, SAP Build Process Automation released standard notifications integrated by default into all running processes.
 
Now, with a simple configuration in BTP, all running tasks automatically generate notifications to the task recipient, keeping them up-to-date on their assignments wherever they are. Build Process Automation notifications use the SAP-standard Alert Notification Service, which means they are automatically sent over email, in-app notification center and mobile. More channels will be added in the future without any extra configuration work needed from administrators.
 
Here is how to get notifications:
  • enable the notifications feature in your BTP cockpit by following the adminstrator documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/enable-notifications 
  • add a task to a process
  • run the process

As you can see, there is no need to specify notification parameters as the default settings already handle the most common cases. In this scenario:
  • the notification will be sent by email and to the bell icon
    the bell icon is the notification center in SAP applications, like SAP Work Zone
  • there is no need to specify recipients twice
    the recipients will be the recipients of the task
  • each user decides if they want mobile notifications
    the recipients of the notification can personalize, to turn off channels like email or notification center, or to turn on notifications for mobile
SAP Build Process Automation processes control finely-tuned sequences of tasks, decisions and applications in order to ensure that company workflows run predictably and error-free. When employees are required to contribute, notifications help get their attention precisely at the moment it is needed.
 
The notifications provide context and quickly let the recipient understand and decide what needs to get done. Opening the tasks in MyInbox or the modern SAP Task Center gives more details and actions. In a soon-to-be-released upgrade, links will point directly to the tasks and the personalization views.

entry.title: Integrating a Makerspace inventory to create an Immersive Experience using Build Code
entry.content:
This year we are celebrating 10 years of d-shop in Newtown Square. d-shop is SAPs internal maker space, a community collaboration that allows employees and visitors alike to experience new technology, share hobbies and turn ideas into reality.

There are ~39 community run d-shops globally withinSAP all with their own local flavor.

We have been fortunate in Newtown Square to have been able to support various conference and user group build projects. This has the wonderful side effect of leaving around a number of physical assets that we can make available to the local community.

We have Robots, Drones, Microcontrollers, xR headsets amongst our inventory. As a community room, we want the equipment we own to be used, not hidden away in cabinets. To enable this we have been using an Inventory Application that visitors can use to find and reserve items. Our original application was d-shop community built and ran on XSC. A maker space is there to enable innovation, so not only did we use it to track inventory and allow checkin/checkout, we also integrated with the physical room. When an item was checked in and out, Hue lighting would react, Amazon Echo and Google Home were added to provide voice interactivity. We tried NFC, QR codes and mobility to enable simple asset tracking.

 

Time has come to for a renewal, we are starting to see increased engagement in the d-shop, 3d printers are running at capacity, workshop requests are increasing. What a better time to take stock of the room and create a new integrated Inventory Application.

This is a journey, we are open to new ideas and integrations as we grow. Some ideas we have don't have any practical application beyond it being fun to try, others provide lots of benefit in both learning and practical applications.
As an example, in the past we created a d-shop coin virtual currency on Hyperledger, the idea was to reward returning equipment with credits. It was a fun group effort building and learning.

This time our plan is to leverage BTP and Build Code to help us rapidly create the new baseline for the inventory application. We are opening this project within the NsQ office to volunteers so others can learn as we build. We plan to share our steps in the community along the journey with our successes, failures, examples and perhaps seeding new ideas from feedback we get in the comments.

First steps, we will work on our MVP requirements and from that create prompts to help generate the first version of the application.

Watch this space....
